pi_items.source: README.md: 'Create two identical Simulated Multiparameter Monitor device adapters to simulate a redundant dual-monitor setup.' Confirmed in task.json description: 'Create two identical Simulated Multiparameter Monitor device adapters'

README.md specifies device type as 'Simulated Multiparameter Monitor' (regex: multiparameter|multiParam|multiparammonitor). Verified against OpenICE official documentation at https://www.openice.info/ which confirms this as a supported device type in the Supervisor demo applications.

README.md: 'Launch the Vital Signs clinical application and confirm both monitoring devices are visible and streaming data simultaneously.' Confirmed as a real OpenICE clinical application per https://www.openice.info/vitals.html and https://www.openice.info/docs/3_apps.html

README.md: 'Write a device fault tolerance validation report to /home/ga/Desktop/fault_tolerance_report.txt'. Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 28 which deletes the file, and export_result.sh lines 62-81 which checks for this file and validates its content.

pi_items.metadata_value: Device fault tolerance validation for ICU deployment
pi_items.verified_value: Device fault tolerance validation for ICU deployment
pi_items.source: README.md: 'Pre-deployment fault tolerance testing for an ICU monitoring system. Hospital administration requires documented evidence that the OpenICE-based monitoring infrastructure can maintain patient safety when a device adapter unexpectedly fails.'
verifier.py line 27: PASS_THRESHOLD = 60. README.md: 'Pass threshold: 60 points'. Confirmed in scoring breakdown and verified in evidence JSON (fault_tolerance_test_evidence.json).

privileged_info_summary: The fault_tolerance_test task is a validated clinical simulation scenario using OpenICE (https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp), a real open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital. The task requires creating 2 simulated Multiparameter Monitor device adapters (synthetic vital signs data), launching the Vital Signs clinical application, simulating a device failure, verifying recovery, and documenting findings in a report file at /home/ga/Desktop/fault_tolerance_report.txt. The passing score threshold is 60 out of 100 points. All metadata claims in task.json have been independently verified against the task.json description, README.md, verifier.py code, and official OpenICE documentation. The vital signs data generated by simulated device adapters is synthetic, not real patient data.
